title: Trajectory-informed tips: extract strategy/recovery/optimization tips from agent traces and inject into future prompts
description: The ANALYZE phase currently compares traces vs skill files and edits the skill .md files when deviations are found. This is coarse-grained: it rewrites instructions rather than accumulating structured operational knowledge. The Trajectory-Informed Memory Generation paper (arXiv 2603.10600) introduces a pattern where raw agent trajectories are decomposed into three tip categories: (1) strategy tips from clean successful runs, (2) recovery tips from failure-then-success sequences, (3) optimization tips from successful-but-slow runs. Tips are stored with embeddings and retrieved at runtime via cosine similarity against the current task description. MindPattern already has agent_notes and agent_notes_embeddings tables, plus the full trace infrastructure. This feature adds a structured tip extraction step after ANALYZE that produces typed tips, stores them in a new agent_tips table, and injects the top-k most relevant tips into each agent's prompt at dispatch time. The paper shows +14.3% scenario goal completion on complex tasks. For MindPattern, this means agents accumulate operational wisdom across runs rather than just having their instructions rewritten.
requirements: Add agent_tips table to memory/db.py with columns: id, agent, tip_type (strategy|recovery|optimization), content, trigger_condition, priority, source_trace_date, embedding BLOB, created_at

Add tip extraction function in orchestrator/analyzer.py that runs after the skill-edit diff: classify each trace as clean-success, failure-recovery, or success-but-slow using deterministic heuristics (exit code, duration vs median, retry patterns)
For each classified trace, use a focused Sonnet prompt to extract 1-3 typed tips with trigger conditions and implementation steps
Store tips with embeddings in agent_tips table; deduplicate against existing tips using 0.85 cosine threshold

Add tip retrieval in orchestrator/agents.py build_agent_prompt(): embed current task context, retrieve top-5 tips by cosine similarity, inject as 'Operational Tips' section between skill content and research context
Add tip consolidation: when tip count per agent exceeds 20, merge near-duplicates and promote high-confidence tips
Log tip injection at INFO level: which tips were injected for which agent
done_criteria: agent_tips table created with proper schema and indexes
After 3+ pipeline runs, agents accumulate tips that persist across runs
Tips are retrieved and injected into agent prompts with measurable relevance (similarity > 0.5)
No regression in agent finding count or quality scores after tip injection
Tip extraction adds < 60 seconds to the ANALYZE phase

design_spec.architecture_impact: Extends the ANALYZE phase with a post-diff tip extraction step. Extends build_agent_prompt() with a tip retrieval + injection step. Adds one new DB table. Does not change the pipeline phase order or any critical paths.

context: Source: 'Trajectory-Informed Memory Generation for Self-Improving Agent Systems' (arXiv 2603.10600). The paper's Trajectory Intelligence Extractor, Decision Attribution Analyzer, and Contextual Learning Generator map directly to MindPattern's existing trace logs + ANALYZE phase. The key insight is that structured tips with trigger conditions are more reusable than wholesale skill file rewrites. MindPattern's agent_notes table provides a foundation but lacks the typed tip schema (strategy/recovery/optimization) and the runtime retrieval-injection loop.
